content:
We built OpenScOut because finding a good open-source project shouldn't be harder than contributing to one

If you've ever tried to make your first (or fiftieth) open-source contribution, you know the drill.

• You find a repository that looks interesting.

• You open the issues tab hoping for a "good first issue."

• The last commit was over a year ago.

• The maintainer hasn't responded in months.

• You close the tab and go back to scrolling GitHub.

After a while, "I'll contribute to open source someday" quietly becomes another unfinished goal.

We got tired of that experience, so we started building OpenScOut—a platform that helps developers discover active, beginner-friendly, and well-maintained open-source projects.

The problem

Finding a project isn't difficult.

Finding one that's active, welcoming, and worth investing your time in is.

Most developers only realize a repository is inactive after they've already spent time understanding the codebase.

What OpenScOut does

🔍 Smart Repository Discovery

Search repositories using filters like language, stars, forks, and recent activity to find projects that match your skills.

🤖 AI Repo Scout

Analyze any GitHub repository and receive an A–F contribution readiness score based on project health, maintainer responsiveness, issue quality, activity, and more.

🎯 GSoC Hub

Browse Google Summer of Code organizations and project ideas from one place instead of jumping across multiple websites.

Why we're building this

We believe developers should spend more time building and contributing than deciding whether a repository is worth contributing to.

OpenScOut is still in active development, and we're building it alongside the community. If you've ever struggled to find the right open-source project, we'd love your feedback.

🚀 Follow the journey

We'll be sharing development updates, new features, and the launch over on LinkedIn.

👉 Follow the journey here:

Follow OpenScOut on LinkedIn

If you have ideas, feature requests, or want early access, leave a comment below—we'd love to hear from you.

Building in public, one repo at a time. 🚀
